title: Methods for expanding and differentiating b cells for producing antibody
abstract: Provided are feeder cell lines that can be used to expand and differentiate B cells in vitro , a method for expanding B cells in vitro comprising culturing the B cells with the feeder cell line, and a method for producing monoclonal antibody in vitro comprising culturing a single B cell with the feeder cell line under sufficient conditions a nd for sufficient time to induce expansion and differentiation of the B cell into a B cell clone secreting antibody.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed is: 1. A method for expanding B cells in vitro, the method comprising: (a) isolating at least one B cell, and (b) culturing the at least one B cell from step (a) with a feeder cell line comprising a stromal cell line modified to express a CD154 polypeptide (or CD40 agonist) and a BLyS polypeptide (or B cell survival factor), in combination with IL-21 and without additional exogenous IL-4, wherein the B cells are cultured with the feeder cell line under sufficient conditions and for a sufficient time to cause the human B cells to expand in number. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein the B cells are human B cells. 3. The method of claim 1 or 2, wherein the IL-21 is added exogenously to the culture. 4. The method of claim 1 or 2, wherein the feeder cells are modified to express an IL-21 polypeptide. 5. The method of any one of claims 1-4, wherein the B cells are expanded at least an average of 10 4 -fold in number. 6. The method of any one of claims 1-5, wherein the culturing of the at least one B cell with the feeder cell line in step (b) is performed in less than 2 weeks. 7. The method of any one of claims 1-6, wherein one B cell is isolated. 8. The method of any one of claims 1-7, wherein the at least one B cell has been exposed to an antigen prior to isolation in step (a). 9. The method of any one of claims 1-8, wherein the culturing of the at least one B cell with the feeder cell line in step (b) is performed in the presence of an antigen. 10. The method of any one of claims 1-9, wherein the culturing of step (b) is performed in the absence of added antigen. 11. The method of any one of claims 1-10, wherein the at least one B cell is isolated by binding to an antigen. 12. The method of any one of claims 1-11, wherein after step (b) at least 10% of the expanded B cells are expressing IgG. 13. The method of any one of claims 1-12, wherein after step (b) at least 2.5% of the expanded B cells are expressing IgA. 14. The method of any one of claims 1-13, wherein the feeder cells are the feeder cells of any one of claims 28-35. 15. A method for producing a monoclonal antibody comprising: (a) isolating B cells; 

 (b) separating the B cells from step (a) into single B cells; (c) culturing the single B cells with a feeder cell line comprising a stromal cell line modified to express a CD154 polypeptide (or CD40 agonist) and a BLyS polypeptide (or B cell survival factor), in combination with IL-21 and without additional exogenous IL-4 to produce a plurality of B cell clones, wherein the single B cells are cultured with the feeder cell line under sufficient conditions and for a sufficient time to cause the single B cells to expand in number and to differentiate into a B cell clone producing a monoclonal antibody. 16. The method of claim 15, further comprising (d) assessing the antigen specificity of at least one of the monoclonal antibodies produced by the plurality of B cell clones. 17. The method of any one of claims 15-16, further comprising (e) purifying at least one of the monoclonal antibodies produced by the plurality of B cell clones. 18. The method of any one of claims 15-17, wherein the B cells have been exposed to an antigen prior to isolation in step (a). 19. The method of any one of claims 15-18, wherein the B cells are human B cells. 20. The method of any one of claim 15-18, wherein the B cells are murine B cells or a subset of murine B cells. 21. The method of any one of claims 15-20, wherein antigen is not included in step (c). 22. The method of any one of claims 15-21, wherein after step (c) at least 10% of the expanded B cells are expressing IgG. 23. The method of any one of claims 15-22, wherein after step (c) at least 2.5% of the expanded B cells are expressing IgA. 24. The method of any one of claims 15-23, wherein the feeder cells are the feeder cells of any one of claims 28-35. 25. A kit for expanding and differentiating mammalian B cells in vitro comprising feeder cells, at least one antibody for isolating B cells from a biological sample and reagents for collecting the B cells from a peripheral blood sample, wherein the feeder cells are the feeder cells of any one of claims 28-35, wherein the antibody is optionally a CD19 antibody. 26. The kit of claim 25, further comprising one or more of reagents necessary for culturing the modified feeder cells and the isolated B cells optionally IL-21. 27. The kit of claim 25 or 26, further comprising one or more reagents for characterization of monoclonal antibodies produced from B cells, optionally an antibody specific for IgM, IgG, IgA, or IgE. 28. A feeder cell line comprising a stromal cell line modified to express a CD154 polypeptide, a BLyS polypeptide, and, optionally, an IL-21 polypeptide. 29. The feeder cell line of claim 28, wherein the stromal cell line that is modified comprises a mesenchymal stromal cell line. 30. The feeder cell line of claim 28, wherein the stromal cell line that is modified comprises a thymic epithelial cell line. 31. The feeder cell line of any one of claims 28-30, wherein the feeder cell line is capable of expanding human B cells to at least an average of 10 4 -fold in number in less than 2 weeks in culture. 32. The feeder cell line of any one of claims 28-31, wherein the stromal cell line comprises an MS-5 cell line. 33. The feeder cell line of any one of claims 28-32, wherein the CD154 polypeptide comprises SEQ ID NO: 68 or a functional fragment or variant thereof. 34. The feeder cell line of any one of claims 28-33, wherein the BLyS polypeptide comprises SEQ ID NO: 69 or a functional fragment or variant thereof. 35. The feeder cell line of any one of claims 28-34, wherein the IL-21 polypeptide comprises SEQ ID NO: 70 or a functional fragment or variant thereof.
